mnemonics: McGraw-Hill: My Guide Creates Real-World Heap & Indexed Lists Logically (A mnemonic for the publisher's common data structure topics: Guides, Heaps, Indexes, Lists, Logic)
keyPoints: McGraw-Hill is a major publisher of computer science textbooks
Their data structures books often cover fundamental concepts like arrays, linked lists, trees, and graphs
They provide standardized terminology and examples used across many computer science curricula
commonMistakes: Confusing McGraw-Hill content with other publishers' approaches to data structures
Assuming all McGraw-Hill books use identical explanations or examples
Overlooking that McGraw-Hill is a publisher, not a specific data structure concept itself
